description: Maps an arbitrary JSON value to its RFC 8785 canonical UTF-8 byte serialization. inputJson is the input value; expected.canonicalUtf8Hex is the canonical byte string as hex; expected.canonicalUtf8 is the same bytes rendered as a UTF-8 string (informational; the bytes are authoritative).

vectors.name: key-ordering
vectors.note: RFC 8785 requires object keys serialize in UTF-16 code-unit order. Both 'a' and 'b' here; canonical output has them sorted.

vectors.name: no-insignificant-whitespace
vectors.note: Spaces, tabs, and newlines between tokens are dropped.

vectors.name: string-escapes-minimal
vectors.note: RFC 8785 escapes only what JSON requires (quote, backslash, control chars). Unicode characters above U+001F serialize as themselves.

vectors.name: key-ordering-utf16-code-units-non-bmp
vectors.note: RFC 8785 §3.2.3 sorts keys by UTF-16 code units. The non-BMP key U+1F600 (surrogate pair 0xD83D 0xDE00) sorts BEFORE the BMP key U+E000 because 0xD83D < 0xE000 as code units; code-point order (0x1F600 > 0xE000) would sort it last. Pins the code-unit form.
